site.name: National CAD Standard
site.tagline: The unified language of design documentation.
site.address: 1735 New York Ave NW, Washington, DC 20006

hero.kicker: Version 7 — Released for the BIM Era
hero.title: One standard. Every drawing. Spoken fluently.
hero.subtitle: The National CAD Standard (NCS7) gives architects, engineers, and the federal government a single, consistent system for organizing, naming, and presenting construction documents — from layer names to sheet sets.

intro.title: Why a national standard?
intro.body: Before the NCS, every firm spoke its own CAD dialect. Layer names, sheet organization, abbreviations, and plotting conventions varied from office to office, making collaboration slow and error-prone. The National CAD Standard unifies these conventions so a drawing produced in one firm reads cleanly in any other — and in every government agency that mandates it.

features.title: Consistent layering
features.body: A predictable, discipline-based layer naming system that scales from a tenant fit-out to a hospital campus.

features.title: Uniform sheet sets
features.body: Standard sheet identification and organization so reviewers always know where to look.

features.title: BIM-ready
features.body: Version 7 aligns traditional CAD conventions with modern BIM workflows and model-based delivery.

about.title: About the National CAD Standard
about.lead: The NCS is a consensus standard maintained by a coalition of the design and construction industry's leading institutions.
about.body: The United States National CAD Standard (NCS) is published through the National Institute of Building Sciences (NIBS) and developed by a consensus process involving the American Institute of Architects (AIA), the Construction Specifications Institute (CSI), the U.S. Coast Guard, the General Services Administration (GSA), and others. It combines several previously independent standards into one coordinated document so that the entire lifecycle of a construction project speaks the same documentation language.

principles.title: Consensus-built
principles.body: Every module is balloted and reviewed by industry stakeholders, not dictated by a single vendor.

principles.title: Vendor-neutral
principles.body: The standard works across AutoCAD, MicroStation, Revit, and any CAD/BIM platform.

principles.title: Government-aligned
principles.body: Adopted and mandated by federal agencies including the GSA and the Department of Defense.

timeline.event: First coordinated draft combining AIA and CSI conventions.
NCS Version 3 adopted widely across federal agencies.
NCS Version 6 introduces expanded plotting and symbology guidance.
NCS Version 7 (NCS7) aligns the standard with BIM delivery.

products.title: General Administration
products.summary: Governance, purpose, scope, and the rules for adopting and maintaining the NCS within an organization.
products.description: The administrative module explains how the National CAD Standard is structured, how to reference it in contracts and specifications, how to document organization-specific deviations, and how the consensus maintenance process works. It is the entry point every firm reads first.

products.title: Uniform Drawing System (UDS)
products.summary: The core of the standard: sheet organization, drawing set order, schedules, notations, and symbols.
products.description: The Uniform Drawing System defines eight integrated modules covering sheet identification, sheet organization, schedules, drafting conventions, terms and abbreviations, symbols, notations, and code conventions. This is the most-used reference on any drafting team.

products.title: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines
products.summary: The discipline-based layer naming system that keeps every layer predictable across firms and platforms.
products.description: The AIA CAD Layer Guidelines specify a structured layer name format — discipline designator, major group, minor group, and status field — so that layers are self-describing and consistent. This module is the backbone of cross-firm CAD collaboration.

products.title: Plotting Guidelines
products.summary: Line weights, pen tables, color-to-weight mapping, and consistent, reproducible plotted output.
products.description: The Plotting Guidelines establish how on-screen colors map to plotted line weights, recommended pen tables, and conventions that guarantee a drawing plotted in one office matches the appearance of the same drawing plotted in another.

products.title: BIM Implementation
products.summary: How NCS conventions map onto model-based delivery, object naming, and BIM execution planning.
products.description: New in Version 7, the BIM Implementation module bridges traditional 2D CAD conventions and model-based workflows. It covers object and parameter naming aligned to the layer guidelines, model file organization, level-of-development references, and how to maintain NCS compliance when documents are generated from a model.

products.title: Symbols & Notations Reference
products.summary: The complete graphic symbol set and standardized notation conventions for every discipline.
products.description: A comprehensive, printable reference of the standardized graphic symbols, reference bubbles, and notation conventions used across architectural, structural, mechanical, electrical, and civil drawings.
